paragraph: Woodcliff Lake is a single-family market through and through. Twenty-six homes sold over six months at a median of $1,357,500, and the data tells a straightforward story: sellers are dictating terms here. Sixty-one percent of homes sold above asking, the sale-to-list ratio sits at 1.01, and the median time on market is 11.5 days. When a home hits the market priced right, multiple offers follow quickly. The range is wide, from $210,000 to $2,200,000, but the bulk of activity clusters around the $1.2 million to $1.6 million band. Condo and townhouse inventory is sparse at only seven sales, and those are moving differently. The median there is $1,320,000, but only 14 percent sold above asking and the median days on market stretched to 27 days. The sale-to-list ratio dipped to 0.98, meaning sellers are accepting slightly below list on average. If you're looking in Woodcliff Lake, the single-family market is the real game, and it's moving fast enough that you'll need to be ready when the right property appears.
